{{Enemy Infobox|Bygone Effigy|2}}{{Power Infobox|Slow|2}}
'''Bygone Effigy''' is an {{2|Elites|Elite}} found in the {{2|Overgrowth}}.

== Pattern ==
{{Intents|Bygone Effigy|2}}
The Bygone Effigy starts asleep and follows this sequence:
# {{Int|Bygone Effigy|Sleep||2}} — does nothing on its first turn.
# {{Int|Bygone Effigy|Wake||2}} — wakes up and gains 10 {{BD|Strength||2}}.
# {{Int|Bygone Effigy|Slashes||2}} every turn after.

==Notes==
*'''Bygone Effigy''''s {{Int|Bygone Effigy|Sleep||2}} phase does not feature an actual {{2|Debuffs|Debuff}} status, unlike {{M|Lagavulin Matriarch||2}}'s {{BD|Asleep||2}} or {{M|Slumbering Beetle||2}}'s {{BD|Slumber||2}}.

*{{BD|Slow||2}} only increases attack damage, which means cards that apply {{2|Poison}}, {{2|Doom}}, or flat damage such as from {{C|Serpent Form||2}} are unaffected.

*When calculating damage for {{BD|Slow||2}}, rounding always reduces damage down to the nearest whole number. For example, a {{C|Strike (Ironclad)||2}} (base 6 damage) will deal: 
**6 damage at 0–1 {{BD|Slow||2}}
**7 damage at 2–3 {{BD|Slow||2}}
**8 damage at 4 {{BD|Slow||2}}
**9 damage at 5–6 {{BD|Slow||2}}
**10 damage at 7–8 {{BD|Slow||2}}
**11 damage at 9 {{BD|Slow||2}}
**12 damage at 10 {{BD|Slow||2}}.

*In the case of {{2|Multiplayer}}, each card played by ''any'' player will increase {{BD|Slow||2}} for the turn. 

==Trivia==
'''Bygone Effigy''''s appearance and abilities are a reference to the {{M|Giant Head||1}} elite from Slay the Spire 1.

== Version History ==
*'''Early Access v0.103.2'''
**BETA changes incorporated into main branch.
* '''Early Access v0.100.0 (BETA)'''
** Nerfed Bygone Effigy: Damage decreased from 15(17) -> 13(15) (still attacks with 10 Strength)
* '''Early Access v0.98'''
** Added Bygone Effigy to the game.
